name: Snow Report 2026-02-28 16:00:01
report:
Good evening, Beast fans, 

It was a beautiful day to be out enjoying the most terrain in the East. The sun popped out for a few appearances, we had tunes blasting on the K-1 patio, and it’s easy to have fun when it’s this nice out and the Beast has every trail to choose from. 

A few early snow showers may linger tomorrow morning, but skies will gradually turn mostly sunny as the day rolls on. Highs will climb to around 20 degrees at the base and 15 degrees at the peak as they’ll fall throughout the day, with northwest winds coming in at 16 – 18 mph.

The slopes delivered packed powder and softer surfaces later on reminding us that Spring isn’t too far down the road. The few areas of hardpack will largely be resurfaced by our grooming crew. The bump skiing was on point and can be found all over with spots like Shorty and Great Bear holding nice consistent mogul sections. Groomers are still the place for perfect carving as the softer surfaces allow for deeper cuts. Check out Rime, Chute, and Cascade for two early cruisers to arc and the later to carve with speed. 

For our groomed terrain, the team is adding Double Dipper, Solitude, Sassafras, and Outer Limits to our collection of smooth trails ready to shred tomorrow. Most of Bear will be resurfaced and is a great spot to get the turns going. Check out our Lifts & Trails Report for our full list of groomers. 

Our snowmaking crew will begin to charge up the system tomorrow morning and you’ll likely see snowguns running around midday. They’ll be on a big push in the coming days, stacking up snow on Superstar to make sure we have the longest season in the East! They’ve done a tremendous job setting us up for a long Spring season, give them a shout of praise for all their hard work if you see crewmembers in the wild. 

Key lifts are scheduled to start spinning at 8:00 a.m. with the Canyon and South Ridge Quads joining at 9:00 a.m.

In our kparks, Dream Maker is firing with 21 features between the upper and lower sections, including a sweet rail section near the base of Bear Mountain. The Stash is the place to be for a more natural feel, with plenty of creative stone and wooden features on the trail and many more in the woods. Over on Ramshead, Timberline continues to shine with 45 features, 5 jumps, and a halfpipe, and Easy Street, Lil’ Stash, and Vale Yard are all dialed in for fun progression options. Check our Terrain Parks Report for the lowdown on each feature. 

Have a fantastic Sunday!

 – Evan

 

Operating Day: 110 

Lifts      

Key lifts operate seven days a week from 9:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on Monday through Friday, and from 8:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on Saturday and Sunday plus Peak Dates*; the Bear Mountain Quad will operate from 7:30 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. every Saturday through the end of March.     

The North Ridge Quad will open at 9:30 a.m. on weekdays and 8:00 a.m. on weekends, the Canyon Quad will spin from 10:00 a.m. to 3:30 p.m. Monday, Wednesday, Friday and 9:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on weekends, and the Sunrise Village Triple will spin until 3:00 p.m. on weekdays and 4:00 p.m. on weekends.      

A note for our snowboarders: Thanks to our new cabins on the Skyeship Gondola, we're asking you to please utilize the snowboard racks outside of each cabin. If the racks are entirely full, please put your boards underneath the seats. This differs from our K-1 Gondola, where all snowboards should be brought inside the cabins. Just remember: outside Skyeship, inside K-1!      

Uphill Travel      

All uphill travel routes are currently open! Please visit killington.com/uphill for specific route information, including a map of each route.

name: Snow Report 2026-02-28 05:52:06
report:
Good morning, Beast fans! 

Today will have a bit of a spring feel to it, but don’t worry about a lack of snow—even as we reach the end of February, we still have 100% of our terrain open, the most in the East. Yesterday was another sunny one out there, as surfaces softened up by the afternoon and led to some playful carving. 

The weather looks to be very mild today, with base temps between the high 20s and the mid 30s all day. Seems like we’ll have a slight inversion in the morning, where temperatures at the peak could be slightly warmer than at the base, but this will only last until the late morning. The morning will also come with some stronger wind gusts, more so at higher elevations. Stay tuned to our Lifts & Trails Report if winds force us to temporarily slow down any lifts. 

Key lifts are scheduled to start spinning at 8:00 a.m. with the exception of our Bear Mountain Quad, which has a 7:30 a.m. start time. If you’re an early bird, start your day at Bear Mountain to get the first laps on the whole mountain. We’ll even treat you to a free coffee or hot chocolate in the Bear Mountain Lodge from 7:00 a.m. - 9:00 a.m. The Canyon Quad and South Ridge Quad will follow up with 9:00 a.m. openings. 

Snow coverage remains great all over the mountain, even in the trees. Julio was a fun advanced option yesterday, while Twister and Squeeze Play remain a great starting point for those looking to dip their toes into the glades. If you’re looking for spring-like bump skiing & riding, head to Great Bear or Upper Wildfire. Groomed trails were still dreamy, even as slightly warmer temps began to create some softer snow on top. Upper Pipe Dream, Upper Bear Claw, and Lower Wildfire were all highlights, and they should still be lots of fun today. 

On the grooming front, we had another busy night, meaning well over half our trails are groomed for you to shred. Our team spent a good amount of time flattening the whales on Superstar, meaning steeps that got attention on Friday night (Downdraft & Double Dipper) will be left alone this evening. Nearly every option by Skyeship base got a groom, including most of Home Stretch plus Touchdown and Field Goal. Middle Ovation will also be smooth once again in the morning. 

Snowmaking is off for the time being while temps sit a little too high, but our crew will be right back at it again on Sunday, continuing to build coverage primarily on Superstar. Not many places can say they’re still making snow in March! 

In our kparks, Dream Maker is firing with 21 features between the upper and lower sections, including a sweet rail section near the base of Bear Mountain. The Stash is the place to be for a more natural feel, with plenty of creative stone and wooden features on the trail and many more in the woods. Over on Ramshead, Timberline continues to shine with 45 features, 5 jumps, and a halfpipe, and Easy Street, Lil’ Stash, and Vale Yard are all dialed in for fun progression options. Check our Terrain Parks Report for the lowdown on each feature. 

Finally, we'll be having a special après experience this afternoon: come by the K-1 Lodge deck from 2:00 p.m. - 5:00 p.m. for a live set from DJ Benny Shreds! If you were a fan of our 200K Shred Day party last week, you'll love the energy on the deck. 

Another fun-filled weekend is on the way. We hope you’ll be here for it! 

 – Evan

name: Snow Report 2026-02-27 05:47:52
report:
Good morning, Beast fam, 

The skies opened into a wide stretch of blue yesterday as the sun poured down on our faces, setting the tone for an all-time day on the slopes with surfaces riding fantastically from top to bottom. Today, the sun is out once again, and we’re still sitting at 100 percent open terrain, offering more skiing and riding than anywhere else in the East.

Today will bring mostly sunny skies with highs near 29 at the base and 23 at the top along with breezy winds of 15 to 20 mph coming from the southwest. 

The snow has had a great texture to lean into these past few days. Yesterday there was plenty of that perfect packed powder with more of a grippy feel making turns even more confident. The bumps have been fun to maneuver on trials like Skyehawk and in the woods, Twister and Squeeze Play were soft and a blast to rip as more intermediate friendly glades. If you’re looking to get into some woods that are more open and still skiing & riding well, Ramshead is your spot. Superstar is covered in that grippy and chalky snowmaking snow that is really fun to play with. Cruise Control once again delivered everything you’d want in a trail, the perfect pitch with reliable packed powder. Check out more of Needles Eye if you haven’t been down there in a bit.

Our grooming team added some big players to the plan in Ovation, Downdraft, and Double Dipper. Head over to Reason for fresh morning corduroy or Ridge Run for steeper carving and check out our Lifts & Trails Report to see the whole list of groomers. Our snowmaking crew had the same plan and fired up the snowguns on Bear Trax and Superstar, with guns covering spots around the K-1 base, Mouse Trap, and Snowdon base. They’ll be turning everything off later today as temps rise and will charge back up sometime on Sunday.

Our key lifts are scheduled to start up at 9:00 a.m. The North Ridge Quad will open at 9:30 a.m., while the South Ridge and Canyon Quads will spin from 10:00 a.m. - 3:30 p.m.

On the kparks front, Dream Maker is firing with 21 features between the upper and lower sections, including a sweet rail section near the base of Bear Mountain, while The Stash remains in great shape with its wooden and stone features plus additional natural hits in the woods. On Ramshead, Timberline continues to shine with 45 features, 5 jumps, and a halfpipe, and Easy Street, Lil’ Stash, and Vale Yard are all dialed in for sweet progression options. Check our Terrain Parks Report for the lowdown on each feature.

Finally, we'll be having a special après experience tomorrow afternoon: come by the K-1 Lodge deck from 2:00 p.m. - 5:00 p.m. for a live set from DJ Benny Shreds! If you were a fan of our 200K Shred Day party last week, you'll love the energy on the deck tomorrow. Hope to see you there!

The Beast remains on fire! 

– Evan

 

Operating Day: 108

Lifts     

Key lifts operate seven days a week from 9:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on Monday through Friday, and from 8:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on Saturday and Sunday plus Peak Dates*; the Bear Mountain Quad will operate from 7:30 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. every Saturday through the end of March.    

The North Ridge Quad will open at 9:30 a.m. on weekdays and 8:00 a.m. on weekends, the Canyon Quad will spin from 10:00 a.m. to 3:30 p.m. Monday, Wednesday, Friday and 9:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on weekends, and the Sunrise Village Triple will spin until 3:00 p.m. on weekdays and 4:00 p.m. on weekends.     

A note for our snowboarders: Thanks to our new cabins on the Skyeship Gondola, we're asking you to please utilize the snowboard racks outside of each cabin. If the racks are entirely full, please put your boards underneath the seats. This differs from our K-1 Gondola, where all snowboards should be brought inside the cabins. Just remember: outside Skyeship, inside K-1!

name: Snow Report 2026-02-26 05:49:12
report:
Good morning, Beast fans, 

The slopes are still skiing and riding beautifully, with packed powder waiting around just about every turn. An early round of flakes dropped an inch of fresh this yesterday morning, adding a little extra sparkle to the surface. Pair that with the solid base our snowmaking crew has been building, and the mountain is staying in prime shape. We are still sitting at 100 percent open terrain with the most skiing and riding offered in the East.

A couple of light snow showers did pass through last night, leaving just a dusting of new stuff. Today starts mostly cloudy before the sun takes over. Temps top out near 18 at the top and 24 at the base with west winds around 17 to 22 mph making things feel a little chillier.

The snow surfaces here have been some of the best, with soft packed powder that loves to grab your edge and dish out smooth carving. The visibility has improved greatly, and our snowmakers have lightened up the guns to help out with that. Cascade was super enjoyable to rip down with grabby snow on a steep trail. Full House has been a great warm up run to build up the arcs and Needle’s Eye was holding fun and powdery moguls. Head over to Bear for consistent and fun riding or to the Canyon for some more challenging options. 

Our grooming team got after 79 trails with many of the same runs involved and Outer Limits plus OL Express added in. Be sure to get on that steep ripper while it’s smooth and primed for carving. Bunny Buster will also have corduroy that can be great for a mellow ride or wall to wall carving action, it's all up to you. 

Our snowmakers lit up Lower Bear Trax and Superstar, with snowguns covering spots around the K-1 base, Mouse Trap, and Snowdon base. They’ve been absolutely crushing it lately, moving the guns around strategically to make snow where the weather gives us the best window and the mountain needs it most.

name: Snow Report 2026-02-25 16:21:11
report:
Good evening, Beast fans, 

The slopes are still skiing and riding beautifully, with packed powder waiting around just about every turn. An early round of flakes dropped an inch of fresh this morning, adding a little extra sparkle to the surface. Pair that with the solid base our snowmaking crew has been building, and the mountain is staying in prime shape. We are still sitting at 100 percent open terrain with the most skiing and riding offered in the East.

Snow showers roll through tonight, with bursts of heavy snow bringing 1 to 2 inches of new snow by morning. Tomorrow starts mostly cloudy before the sun takes over. Temps top out near 18 at the top and 24 at the base with west winds around 17 to 22 mph making things feel a little chillier.

The snow surfaces here have been some of the best, with soft packed powder that loves to grab your edge and dish out smooth carving. The visibility has improved greatly, and our snowmakers have lightened up the guns to help out with that. Cascade was super enjoyable to rip down today with grabby snow on such a steep trail. Full House has been a great warm up run to build up the arcs and Needle’s Eye was holding fun and powdery moguls. Head over to Bear for consistent and fun riding or to the Canyon for some more challenging options. 

Our grooming team is after over 80 trails with many of the same runs involved and Outer Limits plus OL Express added in. Be sure to get on that steep ripper while it’s smooth and primed for carving. Bunny Buster will also have corduroy that can be great for a mellow ride or wall to wall carving action, it's all up to you. 

Our snowmakers plan to light up Lower Bear Trax and Superstar, with snowguns covering spots around the K-1 base, Mouse Trap, and Snowdon base. They’ve been absolutely crushing it lately, moving the guns around strategically to make snow where the weather gives us the best window and the mountain needs it most.

Our key lifts are scheduled to start up at 9:00 a.m. The North Ridge Quad will open at 9:30 a.m., while South Ridge Quad will spin from 10:00 a.m. - 3:30 p.m.

On the kparks front, Dream Maker is firing with 21 features between the upper and lower sections, including a sweet rail section near the base of Bear Mountain, while The Stash remains in great shape with its wooden and stone features plus additional natural hits in the woods. On Ramshead, Timberline continues to shine with 45 features, 5 jumps, and a halfpipe, and Easy Street, Lil’ Stash, and Vale Yard are all dialed in for sweet progression options. Check our Terrain Parks Report for the lowdown on each feature. 

It keeps getting better, get out there! 

– Evan

name: Snow Report 2026-02-25 15:03:25
report:
Good morning, Beast fans, 

It was a bit of a chilly one out there yesterday, but visibility greatly improved after the morning, and the sun was shining through much of the afternoon. Today will bring a hike in temperatures back into the 20s, along with around 1-3" of expected snowfall during the day! 

Temps will start around 15° at the base and increase to around 30° by the end of the day. Wind speeds will be similar but in a different direction, pointing more towards the southwest at the base and at the peak. The increased temps mean exposed skin won’t be as much of an issue with any wind. Snowfall should start during the day and continue into this evening. 

Yesterday’s snow surfaces were fun once visibility improved during the morning. A few areas were somewhat affected by winds, but groomed surfaces were still extremely edge-able everywhere. I checked out North Star on Snowdon and Breakaway on South Ridge since both got a groom last night, and they were both in excellent condition. Breakaway should still be in fine condition to check out (and potentially lap) once the South Ridge Quad spins. 

Our grooming plan was much of the same, with many of our cruisers getting the corduroy treatment. The runout of Breakaway was cleaned up a bit overnight, as well as Flume and Idler. Ongoing snowmaking on Blue Heaven will be pushed out, and Lower Pipe Dream will provide the easiest route to the South Ridge Quad. 

Our snowmaking plan made some tweaks to adjust for the wind. Our crews will be shutting down all the snowguns that don't support Superstar and Skyepeak as the wind direction has shifted from what was forecasted. They aim to get back on Superstar to add more guns and intensity later today and will also turn on Upper Bear Trax.

On the kparks front, Dream Maker is firing with 21 features between the upper and lower sections, including a sweet rail section near the base of Bear Mountain, while The Stash remains in great shape with its wooden and stone features plus additional natural hits in the woods. name: Snow Report 2026-02-24 16:12:24
report:
Good afternoon, Beast fans, 

It was a bit of a chilly one out there today, but visibility greatly improved after the morning, and the sun was shining through much of the afternoon. Tomorrow will bring a hike in temperatures back into the 20s, along with around 1-3" of expected snowfall during the day! 

Temps tomorrow will start around 15° at the base and increase to around 30° by end of day. Wind speeds will be similar to today’s but in a different direction, pointing more towards the southwest at the base and at the peak. The increased temps mean exposed skin won’t be as much of an issue with any wind. Snowfall should start during the day tomorrow and continue into the evening. 

Snow surfaces today were fun once visibility improved during the morning. A few areas were somewhat affected by winds, but groomed surfaces were still extremely edge-able everywhere. I checked out North Star on Snowdon and Breakaway on South Ridge since both got a groom last night, and they were both in excellent condition. Breakaway should still be in fine condition to check out (and potentially lap) once the South Ridge Quad spins again tomorrow. 

Our grooming plan is much of the same as in recent times, with many of our cruisers getting the corduroy treatment by the morning. The runout of Breakaway will be cleaned up a bit overnight, as well as Flume and Idler. Ongoing snowmaking on Blue Heaven will be pushed out a bit, and Lower Pipe Dream will provide the easiest route to the South Ridge Quad. 

Our snowmaking plan remains much of the same, minus some potential tweaks to adjust for changing wind directions. Affected trails will still include Upper Bear Trax, Superstar Headwall, Lower Superstar sections of Great Northern down to Snowdon base, Mouse Trap and Blue Heaven, with more snowguns lighting up Lower Bear Trax and the top of Great Eastern if capacity allows. 

We’ll have 14 lifts to choose from tomorrow, with all of our key lifts scheduled to start up at 9:00 a.m. North Ridge Quad will open at 9:30 a.m., while South Ridge Quad will spin from 10:00 a.m. - 3:30 p.m. as a bonus Wednesday option.  

On the kparks front, Dream Maker is firing with 21 features between the upper and lower sections, including a sweet rail section near the base of Bear Mountain, while The Stash remains in great shape with its wooden and stone features plus additional natural hits in the woods. On Ramshead, Timberline continues to shine with 45 features, 5 jumps, and a halfpipe, and Easy Street, Lil’ Stash, and Vale Yard are all dialed in for sweet progression options. Check our Terrain Parks Report for the lowdown on each feature. 

See you tomorrow for more midweek magic!

- Cullen 

 

Operating Day: 106 

Lifts     

Key lifts operate seven days a week from 9:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on Monday through Friday, and from 8:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on Saturday and Sunday plus Peak Dates*; the Bear Mountain Quad will operate from 7:30 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. every Saturday through the end of March.    

The North Ridge Quad will open at 9:30 a.m. on weekdays and 8:00 a.m. on weekends, the Canyon Quad will spin from 10:00 a.m. to 3:30 p.m. Monday, Wednesday, Friday and 9:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on weekends, and the Sunrise Village Triple will spin until 3:00 p.m. on weekdays and 4:00 p.m. on weekends.     

A note for our snowboarders: Thanks to our new cabins on the Skyeship Gondola, we're asking you to please utilize the snowboard racks outside of each cabin. If the racks are entirely full, please put your boards underneath the seats. This differs from our K-1 Gondola, where all snowboards should be brought inside the cabins. Just remember: outside Skyeship, inside K-1!     

Uphill Travel     

All uphill travel routes are currently open! Please visit killington.com/uphill for specific route information, including a map of each route.  

Before heading up the hill, be sure to review our uphill travel policies and grab your Uphill Travel Pass here. All uphill travelers are required to have an Uphill Travel Pass and armband, which can be picked up at any ticket window. Please keep an eye out for grooming equipment and steer clear of mountain ops during non-operating hours.
